section_name: Contract for Improvement of Boundary Highway
chapter_title: St. Improvements & Assessments in Certain Municipalities
text:
(a) A municipality may contract with another municipality for one municipality to make an improvement to a part of a highway as provided by this chapter and for the other to pay a part of the cost of the improvement if:

(1) the boundary between the municipalities is on or along the highway or the edge of the highway; and

(2) the governing bodies of the municipalities determine the improvement is necessary.

(b) Either municipality may use its money for the improvement under the contract without regard to whether the highway is within the municipality’s limits.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 313. St. Improvements & Assessments in Certain Municipalities
##### § 313.022 — Contract for Improvement of Boundary Highway

(a) A municipality may contract with another municipality for one municipality to make an improvement to a part of a highway as provided by this chapter and for the other to pay a part of the cost of the improvement if:

    (1) the boundary between the municipalities is on or along the highway or the edge of the highway; and

    (2) the governing bodies of the municipalities determine the improvement is necessary.

(b) Either municipality may use its money for the improvement under the contract without regard to whether the highway is within the municipality’s limits.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A municipality may contract with another municipality for one municipality to make an improvement to a part of a highway as provided by this chapter and for the other to pay a part of the cost of the improvement if:
the boundary between the municipalities is on or along the highway or the edge of the highway; and
the governing bodies of the municipalities determine the improvement is necessary.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Either municipality may use its money for the improvement under the contract without regard to whether the highway is within the municipality’s limits.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
